_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 8 - Celine lexicon: models (with three-era + accent attribution) + materials/signature-motifs (Triomphe clasp + Triomphe Canvas) + named colors/signatures

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). THREE eras: Phoebe Philo (2008-2018, accented 'Celine'/Cu00e9line), Hedi Slimane (2018-2024, accent DROPPED to 'CELINE', revived the 1970s Triomphe clasp), Michael Rider (2024-present, 'CELINE'). accent field marks Cu00e9line (Philo) vs CELINE (Slimane/Rider).
types.material: signature motif / leather / finish. The Triomphe clasp (a 1970s archive motif, enlarged + de-linked by Slimane 2018-2019) and the Triomphe Canvas monogram ('first revealed in 1972') are the modern house DNA. Grained 'Drummed' calfskin + smooth calf + exotics + the winged Luggage/Phantom silhouette.
types.color: named color / signature. KEY HONEST FINDING: Celine does NOT publish a per-season color-name lexicon (the Dior/Gucci/YSL pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Colors are plain neutrals (Tan, Black, Camel, Natural); naming weight sits on the MODEL + the MATERIAL/CANVAS, not the color. official_or_descriptive marks each.

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. THE THREE ERAS + ACCENT: Phoebe Philo (2008-2018) created the Luggage/Trapeze/Box/Belt/Trio/Phantom/Cabas/Sangle/Big Bag under the accented 'Cu00e9line'; Hedi Slimane (2018-2024) DROPPED the accent to 'CELINE', revived the 1970s Triomphe clasp + 1972 Triomphe Canvas monogram, and built the 16/Triomphe/Ava/Cuir Triomphe canon; Michael Rider (2024-present, debut SS2026 shown July 2025) is reviving Philo shapes ('New Luggage') under the CELINE wordmark. BRIEF CORRECTION: the brief named 'Marco De Vincenzo' as post-Slimane CD - the actual successor is MICHAEL RIDER (sourced Numero/Vogue/Le Monde/Brown Alumni); De Vincenzo is at Etro, not Celine. COLORS: Celine does NOT name its per-season colors (same finding as Dior/Gucci/YSL) - logged honestly, NOT invented. Five briefed smaller models (Conti, Romy, Folco, Tabou, Ring) did NOT surface in a usable source - logged low/unsourced, NOT fabricated. celine.com NOT scraped (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci/BV/YSL); celine.com product copy + IG/FB official-adjacent posts surfaced as search snippets. No Christie's/Sotheby's Celine page surfaced this run; sources were Spotted Fashion (Philo-era WORKHORSE), Weekly Lux Drop (discontinuation dates WORKHORSE), PurseBlog (Slimane-era WORKHORSE), a+ Singapore (Triomphe heritage).
sources: instagram.com/p/DST2YtygSWd (Phantom Luggage returns as New Luggage, Rider Printemps 2026)

coverage.models: 21 model rows (16 brief-canon sourced incl. Teen Triomphe + 5 briefed-but-unsourced Conti/Romy/Folco/Tabou/Ring logged low not invented + 2 bonus New Luggage/Orb); Philo era 9, Slimane era 6, Rider era 1; debut years sourced for Luggage/Trapeze/Box/Belt/Big Bag/16/Triomphe/Ava/Cuir Triomphe
coverage.materials: 9 motifs/leathers/finishes (Triomphe clasp 1970s + Triomphe Canvas 1972 as the DNA, grained Drummed calf, smooth calf, exotics, suede wings, winged silhouette, Box buckle, Belt knot)
coverage.colors: 5 plain-neutral rows (Tan/Black/Camel/Natural/Brown) + an explicit 'no per-season color lexicon exists' finding. NO invented seasonal color names.

name: Luggage Tote
note: THE Philo It-bag: structured tote with winged sides + a front zip that reads as a face, rigid dual handles; comes Nano/Micro/Mini. Spring 2010, Philo's first It-bag. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (Spring 2010, Nano/Micro/Mini) + WeeklyLuxDrop (2010, winged sides, discontinued March 2025).

name: Trapeze
note: Top-handle bag with wide expandable SUEDE side wings, trapezoidal body, front flap. One of Celine's most popular Philo shapes. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (2010, wide gussets+flap, discontinued) + WeeklyLuxDrop (suede wings, discontinued late 2017).

name: Classic Box
note: Minimalist structured flap with a clean box shape + push-tab/buckle closure, long crossbody strap; came in calf/python/lizard/crocodile; two interior compartments. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (2011, push-tab, calf/python/lizard/croc) + WeeklyLuxDrop (2011, discontinued 2023, phased out for Slimane's Triomphe).

name: Belt Bag
note: Minimalist trapezoid with one top handle + a distinctive KNOTTED BELT detail; flap tucks under the belt; hand/shoulder/crossbody w/ removable strap. Pre-Fall 2014. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (Pre-Fall 2014, knotted belt) + WeeklyLuxDrop (Philo-era, discontinued March 2025).

name: Trio
note: Casual crossbody of THREE detachable zippered pouches worn together or separately (hence 'Trio'); lightweight cult favorite. Exact debut year NOT sourced this run; flagged null not invented. Discontinued ~2021-2022 (WeeklyLuxDrop).

name: Phantom
status: heritage; revived/reimagined under Rider as 'New Luggage' (Printemps 2026)
note: Sister to the Luggage: wide wingspans + an OPEN TOP (no zip face), braided tassel zip on the front, spacious interior. Philo era (~2013); exact debut year NOT sourced this run. IG: Phantom Luggage returns as the 'New Luggage' in Michael Rider's Printemps 2026 debut.

name: Cabas
note: Minimalist soft shopper tote, spacious open interior, clean lines; the everyday Philo tote (one of the most-copied Celine shapes). Exact debut year NOT sourced this run; flagged null not invented.

name: Sangle bucket (Sangle Seau)
note: Drawstring BUCKET bag in grained calfskin or suede with a long shoulder strap; a Philo-era minimalist bucket. Double-sourced: PoshbagBoutique (Sangle, Philo 2008-2018, calfskin) + eBay (Sangle Seau, grained calfskin/suede, shoulder strap). Exact debut year NOT sourced this run.

name: Big Bag
note: Large clean-lined tote with two top handles + a functional LEATHER BELT closure; an instant Philo-era favorite. Winter 2017 Collection (SpottedFashion).

name: 16 / Le 16
note: Slimane's FIRST Celine bag: sleek ladylike top-handle with a 16-TURN LOCK; named after the Maison's Paris HQ at 16 Rue Vivienne; first worn by Lady Gaga; expanded to multiple sizes + soft versions. Double-sourced: PurseBlog (Slimane's first, 16 Rue Vivienne, 16-turn lock, Lady Gaga) + FB/EmQuartier (the 16, in store November 2018).

name: Triomphe
note: Box-shaped bag finished with the enlarged TRIOMPHE CLASP (double-C from the Arc de Triomphe chains); Slimane-era house icon. Introduced 2019 (Classique Triomphe first, Spring 2019 runway). The clasp/monogram is a 1970s Celine archive motif (canvas monogram 'first revealed in 1972'). Double-sourced: PurseBlog (revived, Spring 2019 show, box silhouette) + a+ Singapore (series introduced 2019, Classique first, emblem in archives since 1970s).

name: Teen Triomphe
note: The smallest Triomphe (18.5x14x6cm), deceptively roomy; arguably the most popular of the series. Slimane era (post-2019). a+ Singapore (Teen = smallest of three available sizes, most popular).

name: Ava
note: Relaxed grab-and-go soft shoulder bag with a curved flap; first debuted in TRIOMPHE CANVAS, later calfskin (after Kaia Gerber got a custom brown-leather one); later versions add the Triomphe logo. Among the strongest resale-value retainers (~100%). Introduced 2020 (PurseBlog).

name: Cuir Triomphe
note: Understated line with the Triomphe ETCHED onto the leather or LASER-CUT (no metal clasp); originally crossbodies, expanded to buckets; typically monochromatic. Introduced 2022 (PurseBlog).

name: Besace
note: Slimane-era soft messenger/saddle-style shoulder bag ('besace' = French for a soft cross-body satchel). Briefed model; identity is the silhouette + Triomphe/Cuir treatment. Exact debut year NOT sourced this run; logged low, NOT invented.

name: Bucket Cuir Triomphe
note: The bucket silhouette in the Slimane Cuir Triomphe line; distinct from the Philo Sangle but the same bucket shape. PurseBlog (Cuir Triomphe Bucket bag).

name: Conti
note: Briefed model; did NOT surface in a usable source this run. Logged honestly as an unsourced lead, NOT invented. Queue: celine.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum Celine subforum.

name: Romy
note: Briefed model; did NOT surface in a usable source this run. Logged honestly as an unsourced lead, NOT invented. Queue: celine.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum Celine subforum.

name: Folco
note: Briefed model; did NOT surface in a usable source this run. Logged honestly as an unsourced lead, NOT invented. Queue: celine.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum Celine subforum.

name: Tabou
note: Briefed model; did NOT surface in a usable source this run. Logged honestly as an unsourced lead, NOT invented. Queue: celine.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum Celine subforum.

name: Ring
note: Briefed model; did NOT surface in a usable source this run. Logged honestly as an unsourced lead, NOT invented. Queue: celine.com archive (Chrome) + PurseForum Celine subforum.

name: New Luggage
note: Rider's reimagined Phantom/Luggage in grained calfskin; first introduced by Philo in 2010, returns in Michael Rider's Printemps 2026 debut. IG (official-adjacent reseller post).

name: Orb tote
note: Philo-era smooth-calf tote with golden orb studs, looping top handle, curved flap (an Autumn collection). Bonus heritage model, not in brief; single-source IG, logged low.

name: Triomphe clasp (the emblem)
note: THE Slimane-era signature: the double-C emblem, inspired by + named after the CHAINS around the Arc de Triomphe, used as a large cast metal clasp. A 1970s Celine archive motif (originally small, encased within a link) that Slimane enlarged, de-linked, and made the focal point. Double-sourced: a+ Singapore (in archives since 1970s, Arc de Triomphe chains, enlarged) + PurseBlog (double links forming two Cs, revived by Slimane).

name: Triomphe Canvas
note: The coated-canvas MONOGRAM of repeated Triomphe links, 'first revealed in 1972,' revived by Slimane; usually trimmed in calfskin (e.g. Tan). celine.com product copy (Triomphe canvas reprises the historic monogram first revealed in 1972, link motif inspired by the chain encircling the Arc de Triomphe). PurseBlog (Ava first in Triomphe Canvas).

name: Grained calfskin (Drummed)
note: Celine's pebbled/grained calfskin (the grained finish is called 'Drummed Calfskin'); the standard hide on the Luggage/Belt/Triomphe/Cuir Triomphe. SpottedFashion + IG (grained calfskin); 'Drummed' is beat knowledge.

name: Smooth calfskin
note: The clean polished calf ('shiny/smooth calfskin') on the Classic Box/Triomphe/16. a+ Singapore (Classique/Teen Triomphe in shiny calfskin); SpottedFashion (Box in calfskin).

name: Python / lizard / crocodile
note: The Classic Box + Teen Triomphe run in python/lizard/crocodile; a recurring Celine exotic. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (Box in python/lizard/croc) + a+ Singapore (Teen Triomphe in lizard).

name: Suede (wings)
note: Suede for the expandable side 'wings' of the Trapeze + contrast panels; a Philo-era textural signature. WeeklyLuxDrop (Trapeze expandable suede side wings).

name: Winged Luggage/Phantom silhouette
note: The structured WINGED trapezoidal shape with the face-like front (Luggage = zipped face; Phantom = open top) is itself a house signature shape. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion (winged sides, open-top Phantom) + WeeklyLuxDrop (winged sides, front zip pocket).

name: Box push-tab buckle
note: The Classic Box's minimalist push-tab buckle flap closure is a recognizable Philo-era hardware signature. SpottedFashion (Box, push-tab closure).

name: Belt knot detail
note: The Belt Bag's KNOTTED belt that the flap tucks under is its defining hardware signature. Double-sourced: SpottedFashion + WeeklyLuxDrop (Belt, knotted belt detail).

name: Tan
note: The signature warm mid-brown on the Triomphe Canvas + calfskin trim; the default Triomphe/Ava neutral. celine.com (Classique Triomphe in Triomphe Canvas and Calfskin - TAN). Plain descriptor, NOT a season name.

name: Black / Noir
note: The definitive structured-bag color (Box/Triomphe/16); core to the minimalist Celine read. a+ Singapore/PurseBlog (Triomphe+16 in black); IG (Box in black calfskin). Plain descriptor.

name: Camel
note: The warm tan-brown neutral on the Box + Luggage; a Celine staple. IG (Classic Box in camel brown leather). Plain descriptor.

name: Natural / White / Cream
note: The pale neutral option (e.g. the white Ava); a recurring core option. PurseBlog/24S (Ava in white Triomphe Canvas). Plain descriptor.

name: Brown (Kaia custom)
note: The brown-leather Ava (the Kaia Gerber custom that drove the calfskin Ava); a recurring warm neutral. PurseBlog (Kaia Gerber custom brown-leather Ava). Plain descriptor, NOT a season name.
